articles.title: Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign
articles.description:
Multiple campaigns are using ghost accounts to map GitHub organizations, including their repositories and members.

The post Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops
articles.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Abnormal AI sued by Anthropic, AssuranceAmerica data breach affects 7 million people, NSA brings back TAO.

The post In Other News: DHS Database Hacked, Adobe Boosts Patch Cadence, Canada Disrupts Ransomware Ops appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang
articles.description:
Angelo Martino, a former ransomware negotiator, was sentenced to 70 months for helping the BlackCat/Alphv group.

The post Third US Security Expert Sentenced to Prison for Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force
articles.description:
Both foes and allies have targeted the Balochistan Police force in Pakistan for at least two years, according to SentinelOne.

The post China, India-Linked Hackers Both Targeted Same Pakistani Police Force appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers
articles.description:
The attackers call victims to direct them to phishing websites mirroring Microsoft Entra ID login pages.

The post Okta Warns of Vishing Attacks Targeting Microsoft 365 Customers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage
articles.description:
The backdoor’s destructive capabilities include a standalone wiper, ransomware encryption, and a multi-pass wiping command.

The post GigaWiper Combines Multiple Malware for System-Level Sabotage appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ‘HalluSquatting’ Turns AI Hallucinations Into Botnet Delivery Mechanism
articles.description:
Researchers demonstrate adversarial hallucination squatting against popular AI assistants to achieve remote code execution.

The post ‘HalluSquatting’ Turns AI Hallucinations Into Botnet Delivery Mechanism appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection
articles.description:
A Go module is used to load PowerShell code that fetches a resolver from public dead drops to execute Windows malware.

The post Network of 200 GitHub Repositories Used for Malware Infection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: QIZ Security Raises $17 Million for Cryptographic Governance Platform
articles.description:
The Israeli company has developed a cryptographic posture and post-quantum cryptography management platform.

articles.title: UK Government Rolls Out Agentic AI Defense Plan Alongside Industry Pledge
articles.description:
Two announcements on July 7, 2026, demonstrate the government’s determination to improve the level of cybersecurity within the UK.

The post UK Government Rolls Out Agentic AI Defense Plan Alongside Industry Pledge appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Palo Alto Networks Patches 13 Vulnerabilities
articles.description:
Buffer overflow, DoS, command injection, SSRF, authentication bypass, and other types of vulnerabilities have been found in PAN-OS software.

The post Palo Alto Networks Patches 13 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI
articles.description:
Hackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in a third-party system to access a KDDI email system for ISPs.

The post 12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability ‘GhostLock’ Earns Researchers $92k From Google
articles.description:
Affecting every major distribution since 2011, the Linux kernel vulnerability allows attackers to gain root access.

The post 15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability ‘GhostLock’ Earns Researchers $92k From Google appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Defender ‘RoguePlanet’ Vulnerability
articles.description:
The privilege escalation vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-50656 has been patched with a Microsoft Malware Protection Engine update.

The post Microsoft Patches Defender ‘RoguePlanet’ Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Mount Royal University Confirms Data Stolen in Ransomware Attack
articles.description:
Hackers accessed the institution’s internal network and deleted two drives containing employee, student, and university data.

The post Mount Royal University Confirms Data Stolen in Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique
articles.description:
Wiz has disclosed the details of a new AI coding assistant attack method it has dubbed GhostApproval.

The post AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities
articles.description:
The security refresh resolves 13 use-after-free bugs, including two critical-severity flaws found by Google.

The post Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: 8Layers Raises $2.9 Million for Identity Security Platform
articles.description:
The Spanish startup has closed an extended pre-seed funding round two months after launching its digital identity protection platform.

articles.title: Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices
articles.description:
Tracked as CVE-2026-11405, the vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access a device's web management interface.

The post Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Accenture Confirms Data Breach After Hacker Claims Source Code Theft
articles.description:
The professional services giant says it contained the incident, remediated its source, and experienced no operational or service delivery impact.

The post Accenture Confirms Data Breach After Hacker Claims Source Code Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: China-Linked APT Expands Arsenal With New ‘Leash’ Backdoors
articles.description:
Cisco says the threat actor behind the LapDogs campaign has expanded its SOHO router malware toolkit with LongLeash, DogLeash, and JarLeash backdoors.

The post China-Linked APT Expands Arsenal With New ‘Leash’ Backdoors appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Webinar Today: Why Email Security Keeps Failing
articles.description:
Join the webinar as we break down why email-layer defenses alone can’t keep pace with the modern phishing ecosystem.

The post Webinar Today: Why Email Security Keeps Failing appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Google Dialogflow CX Bug Allowed Attackers to Hijack AI Conversations
articles.description:
The "Rogue Agent" vulnerability could have enabled attackers to silently manipulate AI conversations, exfiltrate data, and compromise every Dialogflow CX agent within the same Google Cloud project.

The post Google Dialogflow CX Bug Allowed Attackers to Hijack AI Conversations appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited ColdFusion, Langflow, Joomla Flaws
articles.description:
Two newly disclosed critical vulnerabilities in Adobe ColdFusion and Langflow join two Joomla extension flaws in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, with federal agencies given until July 10 to patch.

The post CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited ColdFusion, Langflow, Joomla Flaws appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Vulnerability Exposes GitHub Agentic Workflows to Prompt Injection
articles.description:
Researchers show how attackers can use a crafted public GitHub Issue to trick AI-powered workflows into exposing data from private repositories without authentication.

The post Critical Vulnerability Exposes GitHub Agentic Workflows to Prompt Injection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: County Government Reportedly Paid $1 Million to Cyber Extortion Group
articles.description:
The alleged victim, believed to be a small Ohio county, reportedly paid the extortion group to prevent the public release of sensitive stolen data.

The post County Government Reportedly Paid $1 Million to Cyber Extortion Group appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Gitea Flaw Under Active Exploitation, Researchers Warn
articles.description:
Attackers are exploiting the critical Gitea vulnerability CVE-2026-20896 to bypass authentication with a single HTTP header and access vulnerable repositories and secrets.

The post Critical Gitea Flaw Under Active Exploitation, Researchers Warn appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA Reportedly Using Anthropic’s Mythos to Scan Government Software for Flaws
articles.description:
The audits are reportedly being spearheaded by CISA’s Attack Surface Evaluation team, a specialized unit tasked with conducting digital defense assessments and simulated hacking exercises.

The post CISA Reportedly Using Anthropic’s Mythos to Scan Government Software for Flaws appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Adobe ColdFusion Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks
articles.description:
Hackers are exploiting a recently patched critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-48282) in Adobe ColdFusion that carries a CVSS score of 10/10.

The post Critical Adobe ColdFusion Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Using Modular C&C Framework in Cyberattacks
articles.description:
Researchers say the Iran-linked threat actor used an adaptable modular malware framework and compromised IT service providers to reach high-value targets in Israel.

The post Iran-Linked Hackers Using Modular C&C Framework in Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISO Conversations: Tarah Wheeler, Cybersecurity Leader, Thought Leader and Original Thinker
articles.description:
Tarah Wheeler is CISO at TPO Group, a firm that provides cybersecurity consultancy for high-stakes organizations. But despite this elevated position, her journey was far from typical.

The post CISO Conversations: Tarah Wheeler, Cybersecurity Leader, Thought Leader and Original Thinker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Linux Kernel Vulnerability Allows VM Escape on Intel and AMD Systems
articles.description:
The 16-year-old Januscape flaw affects Linux's KVM hypervisor, allowing attackers to escape virtual machines and potentially execute code on the underlying host.

The post Linux Kernel Vulnerability Allows VM Escape on Intel and AMD Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Keyfactor Scores $1 Billion+ Investment for AI, Post-Quantum Security
articles.description:
The investment will accelerate Keyfactor's machine identity, PKI, and cryptographic security platform as enterprises prepare for AI-driven and post-quantum threats.

The post Keyfactor Scores $1 Billion+ Investment for AI, Post-Quantum Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Blogspot-Hosted Payloads Delivered in ‘Veil#Drop’ Attacks
articles.description:
Securonix says the sophisticated framework abuses compromised websites, Blogspot, PowerShell, and fileless techniques to evade detection and deploy the PureLog information stealer.

The post Blogspot-Hosted Payloads Delivered in ‘Veil#Drop’ Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: The Shift Toward Business-Aligned Risk Management
articles.description:
Moving from isolated, technical data to a continuous risk lifecycle can help organizations align security controls with actual business consequences.

articles.title: Armored Likho APT Targeting Government, Electric Power Entities
articles.description:
The threat actor uses modular RATs and information stealers in financially motivated and cyber espionage campaigns.

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Target Open Source Developers in Supply Chain Attacks
articles.description:
The PolinRider campaign has compromised more than 100 legitimate open source packages and repositories to deliver a backdoor and information stealer to developers.

articles.title: Proof-of-Concept Exploit Released for Linux ‘Bad Epoll’ Root Access Vulnerability
articles.description:
Organizations are urged to patch after proof-of-concept code makes the Linux root escalation flaw easier to exploit.

articles.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Trick AI Agents Into Making Crypto Payments
articles.description:
Researchers uncovered two campaigns embedding indirect prompt injections in malicious websites to exploit autonomous AI agents browsing the web.

articles.title: In Other News: Canadian Hacker Jailed, Open Source Zero-Days, Two Sentenced for ATM Jackpotting
articles.description:

articles.title: Agentic AI Used to Conduct Ransomware Attack via Langflow
articles.description:
Attack demonstrates how LLM agents can combine known exploitation techniques with real-time reasoning to automate complex, multi-stage intrusions.

articles.title: Medtronic Data Breach Impacts 3.8 Million People
articles.description:
In April, ShinyHunters accessed the company’s corporate IT systems and stole patients’ personal and medical information.

articles.title: Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker Extradited to US
articles.description:
Prosecutors say 19-year-old Peter Stokes was a member of Scattered Spider, the hacking group linked to more than 100 network intrusions and over $100 million in ransom payments.

The post Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker Extradited to US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Google, FBI Disrupt NetNut Residential Proxy Network Powered by Millions of Devices
articles.description:
NetNut rented access to millions of compromised devices, allowing cybercriminals and nation-state actors to mask their identities during attacks.

The post Google, FBI Disrupt NetNut Residential Proxy Network Powered by Millions of Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Cursor AI Code Editor Flaws Could Lead to OS-Level Remote Code Execution
articles.description:
The DuneSlide vulnerabilities enable zero-click prompt injection attacks that escape Cursor's sandbox and execute arbitrary code on the underlying operating system.

The post Critical Cursor AI Code Editor Flaws Could Lead to OS-Level Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure
articles.description:
Hackers are targeting NetScaler appliances using public PoC code to retrieve arbitrary memory content in the HTTP response.

The post New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: How to Conduct a Successful Audit of AI-Driven Software Development
articles.description:
As AI-generated code becomes commonplace, CISOs need new audit strategies to measure developer practices, govern AI tool usage, and identify software risks before they reach production.

The post How to Conduct a Successful Audit of AI-Driven Software Development appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks
articles.description:
Researchers say credentials harvested from hundreds of thousands of FortiGate firewalls are being used to facilitate ransomware attacks by the INC and Lynx operations.

The post FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm
articles.description:
Anthropic said Tuesday night that its AI model called Claude Fable 5 is now widely available.

articles.title: Cisco Confirms In-the-Wild Exploitation of Unified CM Vulnerability
articles.description:
A PoC exploit has been available since public disclosure, and the first exploitation attempts were observed last week.

articles.title: ‘BioShocking’ Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Stealing Credentials
articles.description:

articles.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Microsoft SharePoint Vulnerability
articles.description:
